syntax_13525.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13525.title: Bits - Bitwise Operation
syntax_13525.syntax_pattern:
%number% | %number% #Copies a bit to the result if it exists in both operands.
%boolean% || %boolean% #If at least one is True, then the truth will be returned.
%number% & %number% #Copies a bit if it exists in any of the operands.
%boolean% && %boolean% #If both operands are True, then true will be returned.
~%number%  #Each bit of the number will be inverted.

syntax_13528.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13528.title: Bits - Shift
syntax_13528.syntax_pattern:
%number% ^^ %number% #Copies a bit if it is set in one operand, but not in both.
%number% << %number% #The value of the left operands moves to the left by the number of bits specified by the right operand.
%number% >> %number% #The value of the right operands moves to the right by the number of bits specified by the left operand.
%number% >>> %number% #The value of the left operands is moved to the right by the number of bits specified by the right operand, and the shifted values are filled with zeros.

syntax_13586.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13586.title: Floodgate - Creating a new form [Custom form]
syntax_13586.description:
It is a form with any elements except buttons. This type of forms allows you to use sliders, switches, input fields, selection fields, etc.
The processing of the form elements is carried out in the section on form result
The created form will be placed in the expression `last created form `

en:
It is a form with any elements except buttons. This type of forms allows you to use sliders, switches, input fields, selection fields, etc.
The processing of the form elements is carried out in the section on form result
The created form will be placed in the expression `last created form `

syntax_13589.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13589.title: Floodgate - Form Button
syntax_13589.description:
The button is a section, the code inside of which will be executed when you click on it. After clicking on the button, the form will be automatically closed (a feature of Bedrock forms).

**Can only be used in ``Modal form``  and ``Simple form`` 
The button with the image can only be used in ``Simple form``**

en:
The button is a section, the code inside of which will be executed when you click on it. After clicking on the button, the form will be automatically closed (a feature of Bedrock forms).

**Can only be used in ``Modal form``  and ``Simple form`` 
The button with the image can only be used in ``Simple form``**

syntax_13590.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13590.title: Floodgate - Description of Modal form
syntax_13590.description: Allows you to specify or get a text description in ``Modal form``. Allows you to specify both in an already created form and in the creation section ``Modal form ``
syntax_13590.syntax_pattern:
form['s] content
content of form 
%form%['s] content
content of %form%

en: Allows you to specify or get a text description in ``Modal form``. Allows you to specify both in an already created form and in the creation section ``Modal form ``

syntax_13591.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13591.title: Floodgate - Custom form Elements
syntax_13591.description: Get the value of the **Custom form Elements** after closing, it is possible only in the section **run on form result**
syntax_13591.syntax_pattern:
#User Input element
#Creates an element in which the user can enter any text. Allows you to specify the initial text inside the field, and the placeholder text

form(-| )input (with name|named) [%string% (with|and) [placeholder] %string%[(, | (with|and) ) [def[ault] [value]] %string%]]

#Choosing from the list
#Creates an element in which the user can select one of the suggested values. Allows you to specify the initial value by index.

form(-| )drop[(-| )]down (with name|named) %string% (with|and) [elements] %strings%[(, | (with|and) ) [def[ault] [(element [index]|index)]] %number%]

#Label, note, text
#Creates an element with text.

form(-| )label [(with (name|title)|named)] %string%

#A slider with a numeric value selection
#Creates an element in the form of a strip with a control in which the user can specify a value. Allows you to specify the minimum and maximum threshold of numbers, the initial value and the slider step.

form(-| )slider (with name|named) %string% [[(with|and) [min[imum] [value]] %number%[(, | (with|and) ) [max[imum] [value]] %number%[(, | (with|and) ) [def[ault] [value]] %number%[(, | (with|and) ) [[step] [value]] %number%]]]]

#A slider with a selection of values
#Creates an element similar to the numeric slider, but with text values. Allows you to specify the initial value by index.

form(-| )(text|step)[(-| )]slider (with name|named) %string% (with|and) [elements] %strings%[(, | (with|and) ) [def[ault] [(element [index]|index)]] %number%]

#The switch element.
#It has only two states, on or off. You can specify the initial state.

form(-| )toggle (with name|named) %string% [(with|and) [def[ault]] [value] %boolean%]

en: Get the value of the **Custom form Elements** after closing, it is possible only in the section **run on form result**

syntax_13592.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13592.title: Floodgate - Get the player inside the form
syntax_13592.description: Use this expression to get the player inside the form, instead of the usual player or any variables.

en: Use this expression to get the player inside the form, instead of the usual player or any variables.

syntax_13593.description:

- custom form
- modal form
- simple form

en:

syntax_13594.syntax_pattern:

syntax_13595.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13595.title: Floodgate - Cancel or allow closing of the form
syntax_13595.description: By default, closing the form is allowed. If you disable it, the form will be reopened after the selection
syntax_13595.syntax_pattern:
cancel [the] form clos(e|ing)
uncancel [the] form clos(e|ing)

en: By default, closing the form is allowed. If you disable it, the form will be reopened after the selection

syntax_13596.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13596.title: Floodgate - Get the reason for closing the form
syntax_13596.description:
This expression can only be used in the section when closing the form 
### Available reasons for closing for comparison:

- close
- (submit|success)
- invalid[ response]

en:
This expression can only be used in the section when closing the form 
### Available reasons for closing for comparison:

- close
- (submit|success)
- invalid[ response]

syntax_13598.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13598.title: Floodgate - Custom form elements
syntax_13598.description:
This expression can only be used in the section when the form is successfully closed 
For button processing ``Modal form``  and ``Simple form``  use the ``button creation section ``

en:
This expression can only be used in the section when the form is successfully closed 
For button processing ``Modal form``  and ``Simple form``  use the ``button creation section ``

syntax_13599.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13599.title: Maps - Creating a canvas
syntax_13599.description:
Returns an object from which, later, you can get a map in the form of an object

**When creating a map in a folder A map image file will be created. The file name will match the ID of the game map. ``/skcrew/maps/``**

en:
Returns an object from which, later, you can get a map in the form of an object

**When creating a map in a folder A map image file will be created. The file name will match the ID of the game map. ``/skcrew/maps/``**

syntax_13602.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13602.title: Maps - Getting a canvas from an object
syntax_13602.description: Accepts a map item as an argument, returns a canvas for drawing

en: Accepts a map item as an argument, returns a canvas for drawing

syntax_13603.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13603.title: Maps - Forced saving of the canvas
syntax_13603.description:
NOTE: Maps are not loaded on their own, so that they do not disappear - put the canvases of these maps in global variables.

The addon supports automatic serialization of data associated with canvases.

en:
NOTE: Maps are not loaded on their own, so that they do not disappear - put the canvases of these maps in global variables.

The addon supports automatic serialization of data associated with canvases.

syntax_13604.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13604.title: Maps - Get the color of a pixel on the canvas
syntax_13604.description: WARNING: It does not allow you to change the color of a pixel, use the expression ``paint over a pixel``

en: WARNING: It does not allow you to change the color of a pixel, use the expression ``paint over a pixel``

syntax_13605.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13605.title: Maps - Paint over a pixel
syntax_13605.description: Allows you to set a specific color for a pixel based on its coordinates. The first two arguments point to the coordinates on the canvas, the subsequent ones are in RGB or RGBA format, and the last one is the canvas.

en: Allows you to set a specific color for a pixel based on its coordinates. The first two arguments point to the coordinates on the canvas, the subsequent ones are in RGB or RGBA format, and the last one is the canvas.

syntax_13606.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13606.title: Maps - Paint over pixels
syntax_13606.description:
Allows you to set a specific color for pixels in the range.
The first four arguments point to the coordinates on the canvas, the next color is in RGB or RGBA format, and the last one is the canvas.

en:
Allows you to set a specific color for pixels in the range.
The first four arguments point to the coordinates on the canvas, the next color is in RGB or RGBA format, and the last one is the canvas.

syntax_13607.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13607.title: Maps - Draw a line
syntax_13607.description: Accepts similar arguments with pixel coloring.

en: Accepts similar arguments with pixel coloring.

syntax_13628.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13628.title: Packets - Get or change the player’s Skin value

syntax_13671.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13671.title: Sockets - Signals - Get the signal key.

syntax_13608.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13608.title: Maps - Draw a bezier curve
syntax_13608.description: The first four arguments are the points between which the main part of the curve will be. The following four arguments allow you to optionally specify control points for the curve offset. The following arguments are similar to drawing a line or coloring pixels.
syntax_13608.syntax_pattern: draw bezier curve (from|between) %number%(,[ ]| )%number% [to] %number%(,[ ]| )%number% [[with] control points] %number%(,[ ]| )%number% [and] %number%(,[ ]| )%number% [with colo[u]r] %number%(,[ ]| )%number%(,[ ]| )%number%[(,[ ]| )%-number%] on [map] %map%

en: The first four arguments are the points between which the main part of the curve will be. The following four arguments allow you to optionally specify control points for the curve offset. The following arguments are similar to drawing a line or coloring pixels.

syntax_13609.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13609.title: Maps - Draw a circle
syntax_13609.description: The first two arguments are the center of the circle, the next one is the radius of the circle. The other arguments are color and canvas.
syntax_13609.syntax_pattern: draw circle at %number%(,[ ]| )%number% with radius %number% [with colo[u]r] %number%(,[ ]| )%number%(,[ ]| )%number%[(,[ ]| )%-number%] on [map] %map%

en: The first two arguments are the center of the circle, the next one is the radius of the circle. The other arguments are color and canvas.

syntax_13610.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13610.title: Maps - Draw an image
syntax_13610.description: The image should be in the images/ folder of the addon. Takes the name of the image file as the first argument.

en: The image should be in the images/ folder of the addon. Takes the name of the image file as the first argument.

syntax_13611.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13611.title: Maps - Write text on the map
syntax_13611.description: The first argument is the text to be written, the next two arguments describe the font name and font size.
syntax_13611.syntax_pattern: draw text %string% [at] %number%(,[ ]| )%number% [with font %-string%] [with size %-number%] [with colo[u]r] %number%(,[ ]| )%number%(,[ ]| )%number%[(,[ ]| )%-number%] on [map] %map%

en: The first argument is the text to be written, the next two arguments describe the font name and font size.

syntax_13624.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13624.title: Packets - Reading the buffer
syntax_13624.description: NOTE: To read utf-8 you must also specify the length of the text in bytes
syntax_13624.syntax_pattern:
read bool[ean] from %bytebuf%
read uuid from %bytebuf%
read string from %bytebuf%
read position from %bytebuf%
read [unsigned] byte from %bytebuf%
read [unsigned] short from %bytebuf%
read float from %bytebuf%
read double from %bytebuf%
read int[eger] from %bytebuf%
read long from %bytebuf%
read angle from %bytebuf%
read var[iable][ ]int[eger] from %bytebuf%
read var[iable][ ]long from %bytebuf%
read utf[(-| )]8 [with [len[gth]]] %number% from %bytebuf%

en: NOTE: To read utf-8 you must also specify the length of the text in bytes

syntax_13625.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13625.title: Packets - Reader index
syntax_13625.description:
Each reading of the buffer shifts its , this is the number of bytes read inside the buffer.
you can find out or change it using the following expression

en:
Each reading of the buffer shifts its , this is the number of bytes read inside the buffer.
you can find out or change it using the following expression

syntax_13614.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13614.title: Category of Damage Type
syntax_13614.description:
## Relating types of damage:

(Environmental - All type of damage)
(Fire - Fire, lava, and blaze fireballs)
(Explosion - Explosions)
(Projectile - Arrows, thrown tridents, ghast and blaze fireballs)
(Fall - Fall damage (including ender pearls))

en:
## Relating types of damage:

(Environmental - All type of damage)
(Fire - Fire, lava, and blaze fireballs)
(Explosion - Explosions)
(Projectile - Arrows, thrown tridents, ghast and blaze fireballs)
(Fall - Fall damage (including ender pearls))

syntax_13615.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13615.title: Other - Inventory drag event
syntax_13615.description: An event triggered when a player presses the mouse button to distribute items in the inventory

en: An event triggered when a player presses the mouse button to distribute items in the inventory

syntax_13618.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13618.title: Packets - Filling the buffer with data
syntax_13618.syntax_pattern:
write bytes %bytebuf% to %bytebuf%
write bool[ean] %boolean% to %bytebuf%
write uuid %string% to %bytebuf%
write string %string% to %bytebuf%
write utf[-| ]8 %string% to %bytebuf%
write position %vector% to %bytebuf%
write position %location% to %bytebuf%
write [unsigned] byte %number% to %bytebuf%
write [unsigned] short %number% to %bytebuf%
write float %number% to %bytebuf%
write double %number% to %bytebuf%
write int[eger] %number% to %bytebuf%
write long %number% to %bytebuf%
write angle %number% to %bytebuf%
write var[iable][ ]int[eger] %number% to %bytebuf%
write var[iable][ ]long %number% to %bytebuf%

syntax_13619.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13619.title: Packets - Write index
syntax_13619.description:
Each write to the buffer shifts its , which is the number of bytes written inside the buffer.
you can find out or change it using the following expression: ``Writer index``

en:
Each write to the buffer shifts its , which is the number of bytes written inside the buffer.
you can find out or change it using the following expression: ``Writer index``

syntax_13620.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13620.title: Packets - Create
syntax_13620.description: The expression below allows you to create a packet based on the packet name and the filled buffer for subsequent sending.

en: The expression below allows you to create a packet based on the packet name and the filled buffer for subsequent sending.

syntax_13626.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13626.title: Packets - Disabling or enabling packet handling
syntax_13626.description: For any player, you can enable or disable tracking of incoming or outgoing packets.

en: For any player, you can enable or disable tracking of incoming or outgoing packets.

syntax_13622.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13622.title: Packets - Handle
syntax_13622.description:
`<packet name>` - allows you to specify the name of the tracking packet.

note: `event-packet` it can be replaced with `set event-packet to %packet%`, provided that no execution delay was used

en:
`<packet name>` - allows you to specify the name of the tracking packet.

note: `event-packet` it can be replaced with `set event-packet to %packet%`, provided that no execution delay was used

syntax_13631.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13631.title: Packets - Get text bytes
syntax_13631.description:
Returns a list of bytes.
It is also possible to specify the required encoding. Default is `UTF-8`

en:
Returns a list of bytes.
It is also possible to specify the required encoding. Default is `UTF-8`

syntax_13632.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13632.title: Packets - Create Bundle packet
syntax_13632.description:
This is a packet that allows you to create a sequence of packets and send them to the client at once, which provides greater speed and stability.
If you are sending many packets at once, put them in a Bundle.

en:
This is a packet that allows you to create a sequence of packets and send them to the client at once, which provides greater speed and stability.
If you are sending many packets at once, put them in a Bundle.

syntax_13635.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13635.title: Requests - Async HTTP/S request
syntax_13635.description: Executes a request with the specified method to the specified site using the specified headers and the specified request body. Returns the code and the body of the response.
syntax_13635.syntax_pattern: [async[hronously]] request [%string%] to [url] %string% [with header[s] %request properties%] [(and|with) body %string%] [and store [[the] (body|result) in %object%] [and] [code in %object%]]

en: Executes a request with the specified method to the specified site using the specified headers and the specified request body. Returns the code and the body of the response.

syntax_13634.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13634.title: Permissions - Execute a command with operator permissions
syntax_13634.syntax_pattern:
[execute] [the] command %strings% [by %-commandsenders%] as op
[execute] [the] %commandsenders% command %strings% as op
(let|make) %commandsenders% execute [[the] command] %strings% as op

syntax_13633.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13633.title: Permissions - Edit
syntax_13633.description: Allows you to delete, view, add permissions to the user without additional plugins

en: Allows you to delete, view, add permissions to the user without additional plugins

syntax_13613.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13613.title: Damage Entity considering armor & potions
syntax_13613.description: The [damage type category](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=13614) is an optional parameter that defines the type of damage affecting the armor. If this parameter is not specified, the environmental damage type will be used by default.
syntax_13613.syntax_pattern: damage %entity% with armor[ and potions] by %number% [heart[s]] [using [(type|cause)] %-damagetypecategory%]

en: The [damage type category](https://skripthub.net/docs/?id=13614) is an optional parameter that defines the type of damage affecting the armor. If this parameter is not specified, the environmental damage type will be used by default.

syntax_13636.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13636.title: Requests - Sync HTTP/S request
syntax_13636.description:
Executes a request with the specified method to the specified site using the specified headers and the specified request body. Returns the code and the body of the response.

WARNING: Block the main thread for the duration of the request, use wisely.
syntax_13636.syntax_pattern: sync[hronously] request [%string%] to [url] %string% [with header[s] %request properties%] [(and|with) body %string%] [and store [[the] (body|result) in %object%] [and] [code in %object%]]

en:
Executes a request with the specified method to the specified site using the specified headers and the specified request body. Returns the code and the body of the response.

WARNING: Block the main thread for the duration of the request, use wisely.

syntax_13641.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13641.title: Runtime - Allocated RAM
syntax_13641.syntax_pattern:
#Server (amount of allocated RAM for this process)

[skcrew] max memory of server
[skcrew] [the] server['s] max memory

#System

[skcrew] max memory of system
[skcrew] [the] system['s] max memory

en: in bytes

syntax_13640.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13640.title: Runtime - Run the command in the operating system
syntax_13640.description: Executes a command in the console/terminal of the operating system.

en: Executes a command in the console/terminal of the operating system.

syntax_13643.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13643.title: Runtime - Used RAM
syntax_13643.syntax_pattern:
#Server (this process)

[skcrew] used memory of server
[skcrew] [the] server['s] used memory

#System

[skcrew] used memory of system
[skcrew] [the] system['s] used memory

en: in bytes

syntax_13621.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13621.title: Packets - Send Packet
syntax_13621.syntax_pattern:
#The following expression is used to send packets:

send packet %packets% to %players%

#The expressions below allow you to send a packet without calling event 'on packet'

send packet %packets%  without [(trigger|call)[ing]] [the] event to %players%
send packet %packets% to %players%  without [(trigger|call)[ing]] [the] event

syntax_13651.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13651.title: Sockets - Get the server by name
syntax_13651.description:
Returns the server by the name under which it is written in the proxy server configuration.
If this server is not listed in the proxy server configuration, <none> will be returned

en:
Returns the server by the name under which it is written in the proxy server configuration.
If this server is not listed in the proxy server configuration, <none> will be returned

syntax_13653.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13653.title: Sockets - Get a list of servers
syntax_13653.description: The expression below returns a list of all servers described in the proxy server configuration.
syntax_13653.syntax_pattern:
[all] servers

#Get only those servers that are enabled. In order for the server to be defined as enabled, it must be connected to a socket server.

[all] online servers

en: The expression below returns a list of all servers described in the proxy server configuration.

syntax_13654.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13654.title: Sockets - Get players from the server
syntax_13654.description: Returns a list of players in the form of online players.

en: Returns a list of players in the form of online players.

syntax_13655.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13655.title: Sockets - Get a player on a proxy server
syntax_13655.description: Accepts the player’s nickname or UUID, returns the online player, or <none> if the player is not online

en: Accepts the player’s nickname or UUID, returns the online player, or <none> if the player is not online

syntax_13658.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13658.title: Sockets - Check that the player is online
syntax_13658.syntax_pattern:
%offlineplayers% is ([online ]on|connected to) proxy
%offlineplayers% (does|is)(n't| not) ([online ]on|connected to) proxy

syntax_13659.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13659.title: Sockets - Kick a player from a proxy server
syntax_13659.description: Allows you to specify the reason. The reason may be plain text, or an AdventureAPI component ( Velocity  ) or ChatComponentAPI ( Bungeecord  ) in JSON format.
syntax_13659.syntax_pattern: kick %offlineplayers% from proxy [(by reason of|because [of]|on account of|due to) %string%]

en: Allows you to specify the reason. The reason may be plain text, or an AdventureAPI component ( Velocity  ) or ChatComponentAPI ( Bungeecord  ) in JSON format.

syntax_13660.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13660.title: Sockets - Move the player to the server
syntax_13660.description: Allows you to change the player’s server if it is located on a server that is connected to a proxy server

en: Allows you to change the player’s server if it is located on a server that is connected to a proxy server

syntax_13661.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13661.title: Sockets - The player has connected to the proxy server
syntax_13661.description:
Called when the player has not yet connected to any server

The event has built-in data, using the event-player you can get the connected player in the form of an OfflinePlayer

en:
Called when the player has not yet connected to any server

The event has built-in data, using the event-player you can get the connected player in the form of an OfflinePlayer

syntax_13662.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13662.title: Sockets - The player disconnected from the proxy server
syntax_13662.description: The event has built-in data, using the event-player you can get the connected player in the form of an OfflinePlayer

en: The event has built-in data, using the event-player you can get the connected player in the form of an OfflinePlayer

syntax_13663.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13663.title: Sockets - The current server has connected to the proxy
syntax_13663.description: Called after the server has been connected to the proxy.
syntax_13663.syntax_pattern:
on connect(ed|s) [to] proxy [server]
on proxy connect

en: Called after the server has been connected to the proxy.

syntax_13664.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13664.title: Sockets - The current server has disconnected from the proxy
syntax_13664.description: Called after the server has been disconnected from the proxy.
syntax_13664.syntax_pattern:
on disconnect(ed|s) [from] proxy [server]
on proxy disconnect

en: Called after the server has been disconnected from the proxy.

syntax_13668.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13668.title: Sockets - The player has connected to the game server
syntax_13668.description: It is called after the player is connected to the game server, which is connected to the proxy.
syntax_13668.syntax_pattern:
on player (join|connect)[(ed|s)] [to] (proxied|network) server

#The expression below allows you to track connections only to a specific server by its name.

on player (join|connect)[(ed|s)] [to] (proxied|network) server %string%

en: It is called after the player is connected to the game server, which is connected to the proxy.

syntax_13667.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13667.title: Sockets - The server was disconnected from the proxy
syntax_13667.description:
Called after any server has disconnected from the proxy

The event has embedded data, using the event-server you can get a disconnected server

en:
Called after any server has disconnected from the proxy

The event has embedded data, using the event-server you can get a disconnected server

syntax_13669.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13669.title: Sockets - The player disconnected from the game server
syntax_13669.syntax_pattern:
on player (leave|disconnect[e])[(d|s)] [from] (proxied|network) server

#The expression below allows you to track disconnections only from a specific server by its name.
on player (leave|disconnect[e])[(d|s)] [from] (proxied|network) server %string%

syntax_13670.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13670.title: Sockets - Signals - Create
syntax_13670.description:
Signals allow you to transfer the information you need between servers.

The signal has the form key : values, which allows you to transfer any data that can be serialized.

en:
Signals allow you to transfer the information you need between servers.

The signal has the form key : values, which allows you to transfer any data that can be serialized.

syntax_13675.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13675.title: SQL - DBMS
syntax_13675.description:
Initializes the connection to the DBMS and returns this connection. Returns an object of the type datasource (com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource class). You can also optionally specify a driver for interaction with the DBMS.
If the driver is not specified, the addon will try to select a driver from the list of available ones (mysql, mssql, oraclesql, sqllite, postgres)

en:
Initializes the connection to the DBMS and returns this connection. Returns an object of the type datasource (com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource class). You can also optionally specify a driver for interaction with the DBMS.
If the driver is not specified, the addon will try to select a driver from the list of available ones (mysql, mssql, oraclesql, sqllite, postgres)

syntax_13711.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13711.title: Files - On File/Directory Change
syntax_13711.syntax_pattern:
[on] (file|dir[ectory]) creation[s] (at|for) [[the] (file[s]|dir[ector(y|ies)]|paths)] %strings%
[on] (file|dir[ectory]) modify[ication][s] (at|for) [[the] (file[s]|dir[ector(y|ies)]|paths)] %strings%
[on] (file|dir[ectory]) deletion[s] (at|for) [[the] (file[s]|dir[ector(y|ies)]|paths)] %strings%
[on] (file|dir[ectory]) overflow[s] (at|for) [[the] (file[s]|dir[ector(y|ies)]|paths)] %strings%
[on] every (file|dir[ectory]) change[s] (at|for) [[the] (file[s]|dir[ector(y|ies)]|paths)] %strings%

syntax_13688.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13688.title: Worlds - Create a world
syntax_13688.syntax_pattern: create world %-string%[ with ][[,] dimension %-worlddimension%][[,] seed %-string%][[,] type %-worldtype%][[,] generator %-string%][[,] structures %-boolean%]

syntax_13677.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13677.title: SQL - Sync request to database
syntax_13677.description: WARNING: Block the main thread for the duration of the request, use wisely.

en: WARNING: Block the main thread for the duration of the request, use wisely.

syntax_13683.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13683.title: String Utils - Split the string every N characters

syntax_13686.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13686.title: Worlds - Load a chunk
syntax_13686.description: It also allows you not to generate a world in a chunk if this chunk has not been generated.

en: It also allows you not to generate a world in a chunk if this chunk has not been generated.

syntax_13696.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13696.title: Worlds - The center of the barrier of the world
syntax_13696.description: This expression supports changing the state

en: This expression supports changing the state

syntax_13697.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13697.title: Worlds - The size of the world barrier
syntax_13697.description: This expression supports changing the state. When changing, it is possible to specify the time in ticks for how long the barrier should change its size

en: This expression supports changing the state. When changing, it is possible to specify the time in ticks for how long the barrier should change its size

syntax_13676.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13676.title: SQL - Async request to database
syntax_13676.description:
The first parameter - is the query being executed.
The second parameter - is the insertion of values for escaping. The first character is ? will be replaced by the first value from the passed list in the second argument, the second ? to the second from the list, etc. 
The third parameter - connecting to a DBMS, an object of type datasource from a previous expression.
The last parameter - where the query result will be placed, you must specify the list variable. After the query, the column names will become indexes of this list, the column values will be stored in a new sub-list with the column name.

WARNING: An asynchronous request cannot be used in functions where a value is returned. The result of an asynchronous request will be none

en:
The first parameter - is the query being executed.
The second parameter - is the insertion of values for escaping. The first character is ? will be replaced by the first value from the passed list in the second argument, the second ? to the second from the list, etc. 
The third parameter - connecting to a DBMS, an object of type datasource from a previous expression.
The last parameter - where the query result will be placed, you must specify the list variable. After the query, the column names will become indexes of this list, the column values will be stored in a new sub-list with the column name.

WARNING: An asynchronous request cannot be used in functions where a value is returned. The result of an asynchronous request will be none

syntax_13731.creator: avesatanaa
syntax_13731.title: Sockets - Network Variable
syntax_13731.description: Variable that is synchronized with all servers connected to the proxy. works as a regular variable in Skript

en: Variable that is synchronized with all servers connected to the proxy. works as a regular variable in Skript
